comments.body:
Call Summary -

The customer Rinaldo wanted to show specific services at the checkout from FedEx and DHL Express through the app. Assisted the customer in setting up the rate automation rules accordingly.

Asked the customer about how the shipments are being handled (like label generation and fulfilment). The customer said they are currently generating and printing labels directly from the carrier portal.
So, showed the process of label generation and printing, pickup request and order fulfilment.

The customer had asked about few queries related to pickup request such as providing additional details for the order pickup. Showed the possibilities through the app.

The customer did not had any further queries in the call. Requested for Review and the customer said they will provide later

Session recording attached

comments.body:
Hello Rinaldo,

Thank you for joining the onboarding session today, it was great connecting with you.

Here is a quick recap of what we covered:

1. Rate automation rules for FedEx and DHL Express, so specific services display at checkout based on your preferences.
2. Label generation and printing directly through the app, as an alternative to generating labels from the carrier portal.
3. Pickup request workflow, including the options available for providing additional details at the time of booking.


As a next step, we encourage you to start generating your labels through the app and explore the pickup request feature for your shipments. This should help save time compared to managing it through the carrier portal separately.

If any questions come up as you use the app, please don't hesitate to write back, we are happy to assist. And when you get a chance, we would truly appreciate a review of your experience.

Best regards,
Sagar K S
PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi



I activated your app for just outside EU orders and I have had a first problem in just few days...

Your app did not provide a shipping rate, and the backup rate was used (you can see that the same customer made a second order 2 hours after the first order and no shipping rate was generated...)

Why is that ? How can we make sure that this will not happen in the future ?



For the last 3 years and thousands of orders with the fedex connector I have had only 1 order that was made with backup shipping rates before.

And with your app I got this problem in the first 10 orders... I noticed when I tested the checkout myself that this happened once but I thought that was just a one-time thing, but it seems that its a real problem...

This needs to be fixed somehow as this is unacceptable.

comments.body:
Hi



I will keep eye on the calculated shipping rates.



I have detecter more problems...



1.
If the shopify order has been edited, then it wont sync the items correctly.

For example, i had a order with 4 items where i replaced 2 with different items. After that PH only synced the 2 new items but not the 2 items that i did not change.

Look at screenshots "product sync 1" and "product sync 2". I tried "reprocess order" also but still only shows 2 items and the wrong sum.
2.
Address line 1 and 2 are not displayed correctly on the generated documents (label, invoice etc.)

Look screenshot "address lines" where you can see the 2 address lines correctly shown.

Now look at the Label and CI pdf-s, the 2 address lines have been merged to one line (with most of the address line 2 not visible) and then its repeted the same way on the address line 2 place.







Best Regards,

Rinaldo Hollat
Eurosec OÜ

Please review it once and let us know if everything looks fine from your end.

Best regards,

Thank you for your patience.

Our development team has investigated and deployed a fix. All new orders where Address Line 1 and Address Line 2 are both filled in will now print correctly on the label and commercial invoice, without the lines being merged or duplicated.

Thanks and regards,
Priyanka

Hello Rinaldo,

Thank you for your patience.

Our team has checked your cases and i would like to address them as below -

1. **Order import cases for Orders in OnHold status in Shopify**
The Order import depends on the Shopify Webhooks. They are triggered as soon as the orders are placed in Shopify.
When orders are placed, the orders are by default coming in Unfulfilled Status and the app starts the import process immediately.
However, in your case, the Shopify Flow is added on the orders to update the order status to OnHold if the product is out of stock.
Now, there are 3 scenarios which can happen which will explain your case -

2. **Address showing twice on the labels**
This case has been addressed by our team.

comments.html_body:
Hello Rinaldo,
 
Thank you for your patience.
 
Our team has checked your cases and i would like to address them as below - 
 

  1. Order import cases for Orders in OnHold status in Shopify
    The Order import depends on the Shopify Webhooks. They are triggered as soon as the orders are placed in Shopify.
    When orders are placed, the orders are by default coming in Unfulfilled Status and the app starts the import process immediately.
    However, in your case, the Shopify Flow is added on the orders to update the order status to OnHold if the product is out of stock.
    Now, there are 3 scenarios which can happen which will explain your case -

    1. If the Shopify Flow updates the order status before the Order Webhooks are triggered, then the order will not be imported in the app.
    2. If the Shopify Flow updates the order status after the Order Webhooks are triggered and all the order details are synced with the app, the order will be available in the app for Processing. This is what happened for the Order 2410 in your case.
    3. If the Shopify Flow updates the order status after the Order Webhooks are triggered and before all the order details (like product info) are synced with the app, the order will be imported without product details. As such, the order will be automatically moved to Not To Ship section in the app. This is what happened for the Order 2446 in your case.


    To address this case, once you make any changes in the Shopify Order, like updating the status to Unfulfilled from OnHold, you can click on Import Orders button in the app All Orders page to manually sync the order in the app.
     

  2. Address showing twice on the labels
    This case has been addressed by our team.
    You can try processing a new order or share a test order with us and we will test the case for you on your store and share the details.
 
Awaiting your response.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
